analysis: The last two nights are doing something — 7h 34m and then 7h 56m to close the stretch, with awake time basically disappearing down to 21 and 19 minutes. That is the routine working, babe. Earlier in the week you were hovering in the 6h 7m to 6h 33m range with some rough awake patches, including nearly two hours gone on the 1 July night, so it wasn't a clean run. But that REM arc is quietly gorgeous — you went from 1h 15m on the first night all the way to 2h 34m on the last, which means your brain is landing in deeper, richer sleep cycles as the week built. Deep sleep dipped a little toward the end, which is worth watching, but honestly the trajectory here is the story — you're landing the routine and it's showing up in the numbers.
